name: DisAPster
summary: Repository indexing entry for the ``DisAPster`` project. Index entry in the KFIOSA catalog. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner. Attribution and provenance only; audit code, releases, and licence before use in the lab or production environment.
algorithms: airodump-ng broad scan with CSV parse (AP + station sections split on 'Station MAC' delimiter, 14-col AP / 6-col client schema)

airmon-ng monitor-mode bring-up with 5-method monitor-interface detection (regex on airmon output, iw dev type, /sys/class/net/*/type==803, iwconfig Mode:Monitor, original-name fallback) for Realtek driver quirks
channel set/read with 4-method fallback chain (iwconfig ioctl, iw set freq MHz, iw set channel, ip link reset) and hardcoded 2.4/5 GHz freq<->channel tables
wash-based WPS fingerprint (version, lock state) via 7s timeout probe filtered by BSSID
PMKID capture-feasibility classifier (check_pmkid) and PMF classifier (check_pmf) — feasibility only, no capture
OUI vendor lookup via ~20-entry hardcoded prefix dict
vulnerability aggregator (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/INFO) and dynamic attack-path recommender (WPS/PMKID/deauth+handshake/TKIP/EAP ordering)
client-to-AP association by case-insensitive BSSID matching with probe-request extraction
produces: EAP downgrade feasibility / open-network rogue-AP potential
numbered recommended attack-path sequence with aircrack-ng/hashcat/reaver command references
novelty: Mostly a thin wrapper — all raw recon is shelled out to airodump-ng (CSV parse) and wash (WPS). No native PMKID/handshake capture (hcxdumptool/hcxpcapngtool are listed but never invoked), no active deauth, no EAP credential capture, no GPS. The genuinely non-baseline work is a pure-Python heuristic analysis layer: a WPA decision-tree classifier (proto/handshake/key-derivation/KRACK/deauth-viability), PMKID-feasibility and PMF classifiers, a severity aggregator, and a context-aware attack-path recommender — i.e. structured interpretation of airodump-ng output rather than new signal collection. The robust monitor-mode/channel fallback chains (Realtek-aware) are a modest engineering novelty over naive airodump-ng wrappers. Net: an airodump-ng/wash wrapper plus a reporting/classification engine, not new recon capability.

trust.warning: External community repo. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use. Catalogued for the AI's awareness; novel algorithms are implemented in core/modules/catalog_recon.py, not shelled out to this repo's binaries.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
